title: Washington's Fable Ban Fuels the Push for Truly Open AI - TCR 06/14/26
description:
Reporting traced Anthropic's worldwide shutdown of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's warning to Treasury officials, a coalition of state attorneys general subpoenaed OpenAI over minors and health data, and KPMG withdrew a flagship agentic-AI report after UBS, the NHS, and Transport for London said its claims about their AI use were fabricated.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-14-2026/

0:00 Anthropic shutdown gets a name and global aftershock
4:53 States become the AI rulemakers
7:16 Meta's AI org turns on itself
9:58 The AI hardware stack splinters across foundries
12:32 The Big Four pull their own AI reports
14:55 Close

This episode covers the named origin and global aftershock of the Anthropic export order, India's debate over sovereign AI and open weights, the state attorneys general subpoena of OpenAI, Meta's 6,500-person Applied AI unit in near-revolt, Google splitting its Icefish TPU across TSMC and Samsung's 2nm line, and NHS England putting Copilot in front of 505,000 staff.

The export-control order that pulled Anthropic's two most capable models offline now carries a name at its origin, with reporting tracing the warning that preceded it to Amazon CEO Andy Jassy, even as a foreign-national clause benches staff across every lab and reignites sovereignty debates from India to Europe. India, the second-largest market for both Anthropic and OpenAI, responded by accelerating toward open-weight and sovereign capacity. Where the federal layer stalls, a coalition of state attorneys general subpoenaed OpenAI over advertising, minors, health-data handling, and model behavior, while statehouses pass targeted disclosure laws. The compute substrate splinters in step, with Google routing its flagship TPU across two foundries, the UK committing £1.1bn to homegrown chips, and Infineon opening a €5bn EU fab. KPMG and EY both withdrew reports their own AI salted with fabrications about real institutions. And NHS England rolled out Microsoft 365 Copilot to 505,000 clinicians and staff after a 30,000-worker trial found average savings of about 43 minutes per person per day.
